behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between source collectors and the summary-input assembler (multi-module state wiring). Unit is too low because the bug can appear only when module outputs are combined. Integration-brake check: (a) no real OS/network needed if source clients are stubbed; (b) no dependence on model output quality; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) orchestration here is deterministic payload assembly, not model decision correctness. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about 'content passed to the summary-generation step', not about real HTTP behavior or LLM-generated content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector/aggregation modules with three deterministic source stubs; execute collection; intercept the payload sent to summary generation; assert all three source sections/fields are present and mapped correctly.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is cross-module error propagation: source adapter failure must be converted into section-level fallback state and carried into report assembly. Unit is too low because this is a handoff invariant across modules. Integration-brake check: (a) no real OS/network required if one adapter stub raises timeout/connection error; (b) correctness does not require real model judgment; (c) no multi-agentic sequence; (d) no LLM decision-governed handoff required by behavior text. Rejected higher levels: behavior text focuses on fallback control flow ('note ... rather than aborting'), not real infra quirks or AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with two healthy source stubs and one failing stub; assert run completes, report contains explicit `data unavailable` marker for the failed section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output fidelity (representation accuracy), which a deterministic stub would mask. This is exactly LLM/ML judgment quality, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one generation quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values; call the real summary model; evaluate whether generated narrative preserves those values (with rubric/semantic checks), repeated across runs to detect non-deterministic misrepresentation.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real external infrastructure: end-to-end collection plus actual email delivery to an inbox. Mocked transport can pass while real SMTP/API auth, routing, or delivery semantics fail (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic: behavior text is delivery success ('reaches ... inbox correctly'), not summary quality/decision correctness; LLM can be stubbed for this assertion. Rejected Workflow: no requirement for multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline against reachable test endpoints and real email delivery path to a controlled mailbox; assert inbox receives one message with expected subject/body structure and intended recipients.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans real infra boundaries: transport outage handling plus persisted local fallback and cross-run retry semantics. In-process mocks can miss real failure modes (connection refusal classes, file permission/path behavior, scheduler/run-boundary persistence). Rejected Workflow: sequence is retry orchestration over runs, but not multiple model decisions. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: With email endpoint intentionally unreachable, run scheduled job and verify report is written to fallback file location; restore email service, trigger next scheduled run, verify persisted report is retried and successfully delivered, and fallback artifact state is updated/cleared as designed.
